hero.h1: We build products that are fast, tested, and built to scale.

duo.pageTitle: Meet the Team — OnePiece Coding | React & Node.js
duo.metaDescription: Lahcen (React & TypeScript) and Mohamed (Node.js & MongoDB) — a coordinated full-stack duo that ships tested, accessible, high-performance products.
duo.subhead: Frontend decisions are shaped by API cost. Backend contracts are designed for minimal over-fetch. We plan both sides together — so nothing gets lost in handoff.
duo.footerNote: No handoff waste: UI components are built against real API contracts, agreed before a single line is written.

lahcen.name: Lahcen Alhiane
lahcen.title: Frontend Lead — React & TypeScript
lahcen.bio: Lahcen builds pixel-accurate, resilient React interfaces. His focus is component architecture that scales, CSS Modules for predictable styles, and accessibility that doesn't get bolted on at the end.
lahcen.resp2: Bundle optimisation and Core Web Vitals

mohamed.name: Mohamed Bouderya
mohamed.title: Backend Lead — Node.js & MongoDB
mohamed.bio: Mohamed designs scalable Node.js APIs and efficient data schemas. He focuses on low-latency endpoints, pragmatic aggregation pipelines, and systems that stay observable under load.

projects.pageTitle: Projects — OnePiece Coding | React & Node.js Case Studies
projects.metaDescription: Full-stack projects by OnePiece Coding: production React frontends, Node.js APIs, tested and optimised for real-world performance. See architecture decisions and tech.
projects.h1: Projects built end-to-end — frontend and backend, together.
projects.lead: Each project below is planned, built, and shipped by both of us. Frontend component decisions are informed by API shape. Backend contracts are written for minimum client payload. No handoff waste.
projects.footerNote: Carrying libraries for features a native API handles just as well? We audit apps and deliver a prioritised remediation plan — no fluff, just impact estimates.

op-blog.title: OP Blog — Production-Ready Full-Stack CMS
op-blog.summary: A full-stack blog platform built for real content operations: public-facing posts, user profile management, and an admin suite for moderation and analytics. Built with a deliberate zero-bloat philosophy — every dependency questioned, every native API preferred.

features.name: Dynamic Profiles
features.description: Users update bios and upload profile photos via Cloudinary. Profile state is managed through Redux Toolkit async thunks with clean loading and error boundaries.

features.name: Zero-Dependency Toasts
features.description: Custom notification system built on CSS keyframe animations and crypto.randomUUID() — no toast library, no extra bundle weight.

features.name: Async State Management
features.description: Redux Toolkit handles all async data flows — fetching, caching, and optimistic updates — without a single external data-fetching library.

features.name: User Moderation
features.description: Full CRUD operations for the user base, protected behind role-based access control.

features.name: Content Governance
features.description: Post and category management designed for scale — with pagination, filters, and bulk actions.

features.name: Live Dashboard
features.description: Real-time platform metrics updated on admin actions — no polling, no unnecessary re-renders.

features.name: Replaced Framer Motion
features.description: Swapped Framer Motion for optimised CSS animations. Result: a measurably smaller bundle with no JavaScript animation overhead at runtime.

features.name: Replaced nanoid
features.description: Removed the nanoid dependency in favour of native crypto.randomUUID() — one fewer package, identical functionality.

features.name: Native-First Philosophy
features.description: We do not reach for npm install for every small feature. Framer Motion → CSS keyframes. nanoid → crypto.randomUUID(). The result is a fast, maintainable codebase that does not drag the browser.

features.name: Complex State, Clean UI
features.description: Redux Toolkit async thunks manage intricate flows — real-time profile updates, multi-layer content moderation — while the UI stays at 60fps with clear loading and error states.

features.name: Full-Stack Coordination
features.description: Mohamed built a secure, well-structured Node.js and MongoDB backend. Lahcen delivered a responsive, TypeScript-first React frontend. Both sides shaped by the same product brief.

services.pageTitle: Services — OnePiece Coding | MVP, Scaling & Audits
services.metaDescription: MVP delivery in weeks, full-stack scaling under load, and prioritised performance audits. OnePiece Coding: outcome-focused engineering with measurable impact.
services.h1: What we build, scale, and fix — and how we do it.
services.lead: Three focused services built around real engineering outcomes: ship a production-ready MVP fast, make an existing product resilient under load, or get a prioritised remediation roadmap for your frontend and backend.

howItWorks.step1Text: — a technical brief to define goals, constraints, and measurable success criteria.
howItWorks.step2Text: — prioritised P0 → P3 fix list with effort and impact estimates.
howItWorks.step3Text: — implementation sprint, or a clean handoff with tests and benchmarks included.

services.footerNote: Pricing and timeline are scoped per engagement. For MVP work we provide a fixed-scope estimate after the scoping call — no open-ended retainers unless you want one.

mvp.title: MVP Delivery
mvp.subtitle: Ship a production-ready product with tight scope and measurable outcomes.
bullets: Opinionated stack: React + TypeScript frontend, Node.js + Express backend, MongoDB or MySQL.
Well-shaped API contracts before implementation — no over-fetch, no last-minute schema changes.
Weeks to first deploy, not months — iterative roadmap with scope-locked milestones.
Production checklist included: logging, monitoring, error boundaries, and deploy pipelines.

scaling.title: Full-Stack Scaling
scaling.subtitle: Make your product handle real traffic — without rewriting it.
bullets: API profiling and bottleneck analysis: slow queries, N+1 patterns, and missing indexes.
Queue-based workers, horizontal Node.js scaling, and autoscaling configuration guidance.
Observability plan: structured logs, lightweight metrics, and actionable alerting.
Infrastructure recommendations sized for predictable costs as traffic grows.

audit.title: Performance Audit & Fix Plan
audit.subtitle: A prioritised remediation roadmap with effort estimates and measurable targets.
bullets: Frontend: bundle analysis, critical render path, code-splitting, and asset optimisation.
Backend: query plans, index gaps, cache strategy, and response payload review.
Concrete P0 → P3 fix list — each item with estimated effort and expected impact.
Optional implementation sprint to apply the highest-priority fixes immediately.

testimonials.pageTitle: Testimonials — Real Clients & Results | OnePiece Coding
testimonials.metaDescription: Real feedback from clients who worked with OnePiece Coding: a driving school, a distribution company, and a graduation project. Honest results, honest opinions.
testimonials.h1: What our clients say — real projects, real feedback.
testimonials.lead: From booking systems to inventory management and graduation projects, we work with clients at every scale. Here is what they said after working with us.
testimonials.footerNote: These are genuine quotes from real clients. We can provide references on request.

ahmed.quote: The booking system they built completely replaced our paper diary. Our students now book their driving lessons online at any hour, and we can see the whole week schedule at a glance. It saved us real time every single day.
ahmed.dotAriaLabel: Go to testimonial by Ahmed Iben Daoud

mustapha.quote: Before this application, tracking stock across our grocery distribution was a constant headache. Now everything is in one place — entries, exits, suppliers, and reports. The team understood our business before they wrote a single line of code.
mustapha.dotAriaLabel: Go to testimonial by Mustapha Bougermez

asma.quote: My graduation project needed to handle job applications end-to-end — resume uploads, company review, and admin approval flows. They built exactly what I specified, the code was clean enough to present to my jury, and I defended it successfully.
asma.dotAriaLabel: Go to testimonial by Asma Ben Baali
